title: CoinEx Denies Claims It Served as $3.84 Billion Gateway to Sanctioned Iranian Crypto Firms
summary: Blockchain analytics firm TRM Labs alleges CoinEx facilitated over $3.84 billion in blockchain-traced flows with sanctioned Iranian crypto entities. CoinEx disputes the findings, calling the data incomplete and reaffirming its compliance measures.
body:
Blockchain analytics firm TRM Labs has accused CoinEx of facilitating more than $3.84 billion in blockchain-traced flows with sanctioned Iranian crypto entities. The firm's report suggests that CoinEx may have served as a financial gateway for these transactions, which are prohibited under international sanctions.

CoinEx has strongly denied the allegations, stating that the exchange adheres to strict compliance measures and regulatory requirements. The exchange claims that TRM Labs' findings are based on incomplete data and do not accurately reflect the nature of the transactions in question. CoinEx has pledged to cooperate with any regulatory investigations to clear its name.

This dispute underscores the challenges faced by crypto exchanges in navigating complex international sanctions. For everyday users, it serves as a reminder of the importance of choosing exchanges with robust compliance measures to avoid potential legal risks. The outcome of this dispute could have broader implications for the crypto industry's relationship with regulatory bodies.

Users should watch for any regulatory actions or statements from CoinEx and TRM Labs in the coming weeks. If confirmed, this could lead to stricter scrutiny of crypto exchanges and their compliance practices. Users are advised to stay informed about the developments and choose exchanges with a proven track record of regulatory compliance.
twitterThread: CoinEx denies facilitating $3.84B in crypto transactions with sanctioned Iranian firms, despite claims by TRM Labs. The exchange says the data is incomplete. What does this mean for exchange compliance?
TRM Labs alleges CoinEx processed $3.84B with sanctioned entities, but CoinEx disputes the findings and reaffirms its compliance measures. Crypto compliance is under the microscope.

This dispute highlights the importance of choosing compliant exchanges. Watch for regulatory updates and stay informed. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-25-coinex-denies-384b-crypto-transactions-with-sanctioned-iranian-firms
standaloneTweet: CoinEx denies claims it served as a $3.84B gateway to sanctioned Iranian crypto firms, but the dispute raises questions about exchange compliance and user safety. Stay informed.
